Stu Smith (@thestustustudio)

These are the kinds of protest photos that instantly become historical record. Amir Balat lighting the bomb in one frame, and an NYPD officer mid-air over the fence in the next, already in pursuit.

Rarely do still images preserve both the act itself and the split-second response this clearly.

posts.text:
God Bless the NYPD and all law enforcement. 

The police chief shown in the iconic photos (mid-air jumping the fence in pursuit right after Amir Balat lit/attempted to deploy the explosive device) is NYPD Chief Aaron Edwards, who holds the rank of Assistant Chief, specifically assigned to Patrol Borough Manhattan North at the time. 

Multiple sources, including eyewitness accounts on X and official statements, identify him as the one who vaulted the barricade/fence to chase down the suspect duri
